title: SMP Mods Server
description: A easy to install server for a HermitCraft style experience.
body:
# SMP Mods
This is the server pack for SMP Mods, a group of mod packs made to enhance the SMP experience and make it more enjoyable.

This pack is built upon the mods in the [Hermitcraft](https://hermitcraft.com) server with a few additional mods and added features.

## Features of the SMP Mods

- [**Simple Voice Chat:**](https://modrinth.com/plugin/simple-voice-chat) Adds proximity chat to Minecraft.
- [**Audio Player:**](https://modrinth.com/mod/audioplayer) Allows you to add custom discs and horns using [Simple VC](https://modrinth.com/plugin/simple-voice-chat).
- [**Status:**](https://modrinth.com/mod/status) A mod that allows you to set your status.
- [**Elytra Trims:**](https://modrinth.com/mod/elytra-trims) Allows you to trim your elytra.
- [**Styled Nicknames:**](https://modrinth.com/mod/styled-nicknames) Allows you to set your nickname.
- [**Vanilla Tweaks Datapacks:**](https://vanillatweaks.net/share/#7cxibr) Check out the datapacks.
- [**Vanilla Tweaks Craft Tweaks:**](https://vanillatweaks.net/share/#nxE4xE) Check out the craft tweaks.
- **Performance Mods:** I also added some performance mods.

Players can still join without the client pack, but the features added by the server pack won't work without it. So, you should probably have your players install it: [SMP Mods Client](https://modrinth.com/modpack/smp-mods-client).

## How to Set Up SMP Mods On fork. (windows only)

1. **Download and Install Fork**  
   First, download Fork from [here](https://www.fork.gg) and run the `.exe` file to install it. 

2. **Install Java**  
   After installing Fork, you may be prompted to install Java. Click on one of the provided links and follow the instructions to install Java. I recommend using the Microsoft link, as it’s the easiest option. Once Java is installed, restart Fork.

3. **Download SMP Mods Server Files**  
   Next, download the `.zip` file of the SMP Mods server. **Important:** Do not download the `.mrpack` file, as it’s not the same. Make sure to get the `.zip` file directly from the version page.

4. **Extract the SMP Mods Files**  
   Navigate to your downloads folder, right-click on the downloaded `.zip` file, and select `Extract All...` Click `Extract` and wait for the process to complete.

5. **Copy the Extracted Files**  
   Once the extraction is complete, select all the files and press `Ctrl + C` to copy them.

6. **Create a Fabric Server in Fork**  
   Open Fork and click "Create A Server." Select "Fabric" as your server type, and make sure the version matches the SMP Mods version you downloaded. Name your server and click the anvil icon on the left to create it. **Note:** We’re not using Fork’s "Import Server" option because it can be buggy. You can ignore all other settings, as they’ll be reset when we import the SMP Mods files. You can adjust them later if needed.

7. **Import SMP Mods Files into Fork**  
   On the left side of Fork, click the gear icon. In the settings menu, you'll see blue text that says "Open in File Explorer." Click it. Select everything in that folder, delete it, and then press `Ctrl + V` to paste the SMP Mods files.

8. **Start Your Server**  
   You can now exit File Explorer and start your server by clicking the play button in Fork.

9. **Find the IP and Allow Friends to Join**  
   To find your server's IP address and configure it for friends to join, follow this tutorial: [YouTube Guide](https://youtu.be/YJbhRkE1Q-Y?si=XLfKFtta9DXCVYGK&t=60).


## How to Set Up SMP Mods on ATLauncher

1. **Download and Install ATLauncher**  
   First, download ATLauncher from [here](https://atlauncher.com/downloads). If you need help installing it, you can follow this [tutorial](https://youtu.be/O0iMhGqHkgY?si=W14KIch-LExVHq2Z&t=88).

2. **Find SMP Mods Server**  
   Once ATLauncher is installed and opened, navigate to the "Packs" menu and search for the SMP Mods Server pack.

3. **Create the Server**  
   When you find the SMP Mods Server pack, click "Create Server."  
   - A pop-up will appear asking if you want to create a server; click "Yes."

4. **Name the Server and Choose a Version**  
   You’ll be prompted to name your server and select the version you want to install. After naming and selecting the version, click "Install."

5. **Download the Server**  
   ATLauncher will begin downloading the necessary files for your server. Once the download is complete, click "OK" to proceed.

6. **Open the Server Folder**  
   Go to the "Servers" menu in ATLauncher, find your newly created server, and click "Open Folder."

7. **Accept the EULA**  
   In the server folder, locate the `eula.txt` file and double-click it to open.  
   - Find the line that says `eula=false` and change it to `eula=true`.  
   - Save the file by pressing `Ctrl + S` on Windows or `Command + S` on Mac.

8. **Launch the Server**  
   Close the text editor and the server folder. Then, go back to ATLauncher and click "Launch" to start your server.

9. **Configure Server IP for Friends**  
   To find your server's IP address and make it accessible to friends, follow this [tutorial](https://youtu.be/YJbhRkE1Q-Y?si=XLfKFtta9DXCVYGK&t=60).
